contextBlurb: Rhode Island legislators are pushing on multiple fronts simultaneously, with paired House and Senate bills targeting AI transparency in health insurance and mental health care, safety guardrails for AI companion apps, and data center cost accountability — a notably broad legislative agenda for a small state. The data center bills (S2776/H7331) would require developers to fund grid infrastructure upgrades rather than passing those costs to ratepayers, while companion tax incentive bills (S2346/H7695) pull in the opposite direction to attract investment. All active legislation remains in committee, leaving Rhode Island without any enacted AI or data center law so far.
